demographics.living_situation: resident in a residential aged-care facility; baseline mobilises with a frame

contact_nok.relationship: residential aged-care nursing staff (present and calling on the resident's behalf)

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: One of our residents has been constantly soiling herself for three days now — runny stool just leaks out and she doesn't even feel it happening, it's all through the bed and the pads. The skin around her bottom is red raw and burning and she can't bear to sit down. We've been giving her Imodium for the diarrhoea but it's not settling at all.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The continuous leakage and the raw, painful skin have become a hygiene and dignity crisis and the staff want advice before the next in-hours period.

symptom_narrative.duration: continuous for three days and not settling
symptom_narrative.character: mostly a passive seepage of liquid/runny stool that she has no awareness of until the pad is checked; now and then a sudden strong urge to go but she cannot get to the toilet in time
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: staff describe it as constant and very distressing; the perianal skin is raw and burning
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: bowels and the skin around the bottom
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: continuous through the day and night, soiling pads and bed linen
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing so far; the anti-diarrhoeal has not helped
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: severe perianal skin redness, breakdown and burning pain; she has become bedbound because sitting is too painful and she is frightened of soiling herself
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: facility charts note she had been increasingly constipated over the preceding two weeks before this leakage began
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: acutely bedbound from perianal pain and fear of soiling; normally mobilises with a frame

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: worsening constipation over the past couple of weeks, per the facility bowel charts
past_medical_history.when_diagnosed: documented over the preceding two weeks
past_medical_history.how_managed: no regular bowel regimen recorded before this episode

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: frail, needs a frame to walk at baseline

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: loperamide (Imodium), started by staff about 48 hours ago for the presumed diarrhoea

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: long-term resident of a residential aged-care facility; nursing staff provide day-to-day care and are the informant for this call

communication_style.typical_message_style: nursing staff reporting on behalf of a distressed, frail resident

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: facility staff reading a standard thermometer; not independently verified

objective_data_offered.value: oxygen saturation 98 percent on room air
staff report continuous liquid stool leaking into the pads without the resident being aware, multiple heavily soiled pads bagged in the room, and a strong faecal odour
staff report the skin around the anus looks very red, raw and broken from the constant soiling
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: a lay/nursing visual description of the skin, not a clinician wound assessment or staging

objective_data_offered.value: resident is visibly distressed and is lying on her side to keep pressure off her buttocks
